jokes.text: You people make me sick! Hi, Bender. All happy and cozy.
jokes.setup: Bender expressing disgust at a couple's happiness
jokes.punchline: Immediately greeting them cheerfully, contradicting his disgust
jokes.explanation: Classic Bender contradiction - hating happiness while being social. Good character integration and economy.

jokes.text: We're just so different. / We're just so similar. What?!
jokes.setup: Couple explaining their compatibility with opposing statements
jokes.punchline: Immediate contradiction and confusion about their own relationship
jokes.explanation: Brilliant setup showing how couples can be delusional about their dynamics. Perfect structure and timing.

jokes.text: We're like two bats in a belfry. No, we're not.
jokes.setup: Fry using a cliche to describe their similarity
jokes.punchline: Leela immediately contradicting, proving they're not similar
jokes.explanation: Solid follow-up to the similarity theme, showing their obliviousness continues.

jokes.text: I'm takin' note of the exact moment I lost interest.
jokes.punchline: Bender's clinical observation of his own boredom
jokes.explanation: Excellent Bender line - precise, antisocial, and relatable. Great economy and character voice.

jokes.text: Me neither. We're so similar in that way.
jokes.punchline: Finding similarity in not going to the same place
jokes.explanation: Continues the running theme but less impactful than the setup.

jokes.text: That place sucks burps.
jokes.punchline: Creative vulgar description combining 'sucks' and 'burps'
jokes.explanation: Inventive Bender-esque insult. Good wordplay that fits his crude character.

jokes.text: Sorry. You can't leave unless you know the password. Haw-haw!
jokes.punchline: Absurd rule that you can't leave without knowing it
jokes.explanation: Clever absurdist twist on club exclusivity. Original concept with good execution.

jokes.text: Good thing those spikes retracted at the last second as far as I know.
jokes.punchline: Uncertain whether he actually survived
jokes.explanation: Perfect Fry line - naive optimism mixed with existential uncertainty. Excellent dark comedy.

jokes.text: Oh no, it's the Robot Devil! Can I get a Flaming Navel?
jokes.punchline: Immediately ordering a drink despite the fear
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Fry's priorities and social obliviousness.

jokes.text: No, we have completely different tastes. I'll have, uh... the Flaming Navel.
jokes.punchline: Ordering the exact same drink
jokes.explanation: Continues the similarity/difference theme with perfect comedic timing.

jokes.text: It is? Then, uh, no umbrella for me.
jokes.setup: Realizing she ordered the same drink
jokes.punchline: Desperately trying to create a difference with tiny modification
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation of the similarity gag. Perfect character moment showing stubborn denial.

jokes.text: That'll be 10 more dollars for the same total amount of liquor!
jokes.setup: Two separate orders for identical drinks
jokes.punchline: Pointing out the absurd economics with glee
jokes.explanation: Smart economic joke that fits the Robot Devil's evil capitalism theme.

jokes.text: Who, me? / Who, me? -I'm afraid not. / I'm afraid not.
jokes.punchline: Revealing they're conjoined twins speaking in unison
jokes.explanation: Visual gag that works well in context of Bender's dating failures.

jokes.text: I've always wanted to put the moves on a world-famous whatever she does, if anything.
jokes.punchline: Admitting he has no idea what makes her famous
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender line - shallow celebrity obsession with brutal honesty. Excellent craft and character integration.

jokes.text: Hey, baby. Is it hot in Hell... or is it just you?
jokes.punchline: Twist referencing they're literally in Hell
jokes.explanation: Decent twist on classic pickup line, enhanced by Hell setting.

jokes.text: Is somebody talking words? I, I thought I heard, like, a word noise.
jokes.punchline: Complete dismissal treating his speech as background noise
jokes.explanation: Brutal rejection that reduces Bender to meaningless noise. Good cringe comedy.

jokes.text: Many ladies say my noises are loud, but deadly.
jokes.punchline: Misinterpreting as compliment about his 'performance'
jokes.explanation: Typical Bender sexual boasting that misses the point of the insult.

jokes.text: It sounds like a squ-wirrel or something. Someone throw it a hot wing.
jokes.setup: Continuing to dismiss Bender as animal noise
jokes.punchline: Suggesting feeding him like a pest
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the dehumanization theme. Solid cringe comedy.

jokes.text: My neck joint doesn't tilt down that far.
jokes.punchline: Literal mechanical limitation revealing he's very short
jokes.explanation: Clever reveal of Bender's height issue through robot mechanics. Good visual comedy.

jokes.text: No, no, no, no, no, no. Oh, wait. Short? Yes. I thought you said smart.
jokes.punchline: Fry's confusion between 'short' and 'smart' with brutal honesty
jokes.explanation: Perfect Fry moment - well-meaning but accidentally devastating. Great structure and character voice.

jokes.text: I thought I was one of the tallest guys in the world, and now it turns out I'm short?!
jokes.punchline: Extreme overestimation revealing complete self-unawareness
jokes.explanation: Good character revelation showing Bender's massive ego and self-delusion.

jokes.text: Don't you think I've thought of that?! -Yes. -Well, I didn't!
jokes.setup: Professor asking obvious question about extendo-legs
jokes.punchline: Bender admitting he didn't think of obvious solution
jokes.explanation: Brilliant subversion of expectations. Perfect economy and character-driven humor.

jokes.text: Statistically, short men earn 12% less and are 23% less respected.
jokes.setup: Trying to console Bender about being short
jokes.punchline: Providing depressing statistics instead of comfort
jokes.explanation: Good Professor moment - scientifically accurate but socially inappropriate.

jokes.text: Cork it, stumpy! I'm docking your pay.
jokes.punchline: Hermes immediately proving the point by discriminating
jokes.explanation: Perfect immediate proof of the discrimination theory. Great timing and character consistency.

jokes.text: You probably couldn't see it up on that not-very-high shelf.
jokes.punchline: Passive-aggressive height insult disguised as helpfulness
jokes.explanation: Continues the height discrimination theme with backhanded comment.

jokes.text: Leela and I are exactly the same height. / Completely different heights.
jokes.setup: Trying to show height doesn't matter in relationships
jokes.punchline: Continuing their delusion about similarity/difference
jokes.explanation: Solid callback to earlier relationship confusion, maintaining consistency.

jokes.text: I have had it up to... ...here!
jokes.setup: Classic 'had it up to here' gesture
jokes.punchline: His 'here' is very low due to his shortness
jokes.explanation: Clever visual gag playing on the idiom and his height. Good physical comedy.

jokes.text: Vertical stripes. Mm-hmm.
jokes.setup: Scruffy gives fashion advice for looking tall
jokes.punchline: His confident delivery of basic fashion knowledge
jokes.explanation: Perfect Scruffy moment - treating mundane knowledge as wisdom. Great character integration and economy.

jokes.text: Dang! That's the tallest short guy I've ever seen.
jokes.setup: Bender trying vertical stripes to look taller
jokes.punchline: Backhanded compliment acknowledging he's still short
jokes.explanation: Decent observational humor about the ineffectiveness of fashion tricks.

jokes.text: It's Wembanyama. / Wembanyoyo? / Eh.
jokes.punchline: Bender's creative mispronunciations
jokes.explanation: Light wordplay on current NBA player's name. Topical but not exceptional.

jokes.text: The true measure of a man is not his physical stature. Not when you're short, it ain't.
jokes.punchline: Bender's cynical rejection of the wisdom
jokes.explanation: Good subversion of feel-good advice. Perfect Bender cynicism.

jokes.text: People just assume you're great at basketball. Even if you are!
jokes.punchline: Admitting the assumption is correct
jokes.explanation: Mild self-aware humor about stereotypes being true.

jokes.text: Life up here is legit rough, my friend, but it's cool. Damn cool. And not that rough.
jokes.setup: Describing the hardships of being tall
jokes.punchline: Immediately contradicting himself about how rough it is
jokes.explanation: Shows character backing down from complaint, revealing tall privilege.

jokes.text: Oops, I forgot my IOUs. Will you accept an IOIOU?
jokes.setup: Owing soul IOUs to Robot Devil
jokes.punchline: Creating an IOU for the IOU - recursive debt
jokes.explanation: Brilliant wordplay and concept. Recursive debt is both clever and in-character for Bender.

jokes.text: Have you heard of... health insurance?
jokes.setup: Robot Devil moving to new evil business
jokes.punchline: American healthcare system as ultimate evil
jokes.explanation: Sharp social commentary perfectly suited to the Robot Devil character. Dark but earned.

jokes.text: It's a basic right of everyone in our society, except humans.
jokes.punchline: Humans being excluded from basic rights
jokes.explanation: Continues healthcare satire with dystopian twist fitting the show's universe.

jokes.text: Your copay is three cents, but I'll bill your insurance $27,000!
jokes.punchline: Extreme markup showing insurance fraud
jokes.explanation: Realistic healthcare criticism with absurd but believable numbers.

jokes.text: I feel stupid for askin'. These are D-roids, digital steroids.
jokes.punchline: Robot-specific steroid pun
jokes.explanation: Clever tech-medical pun that fits the sci-fi setting well.

jokes.text: Side effects include dry, rusty eyes, droid rage, antenna shrinkage, loose or watery bricks, athlete's ass...
jokes.punchline: Robot-specific side effects getting increasingly absurd
jokes.explanation: Good pharmaceutical ad parody with creative robot-specific conditions.

jokes.text: Ask your devil if D-roids are right for you. Questions? Yeah. Are D-roids right for me?
jokes.punchline: Bender asking the exact suggested question immediately
jokes.explanation: Perfect timing and literalism from Bender. Good pharmaceutical parody conclusion.

jokes.text: Hey, I've always wanted to hit my head on a doorway, and now I can.
jokes.punchline: Finding joy in typically annoying tall person problem
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Bender's twisted perspective on 'problems.'

jokes.text: I'm 40% taller!
jokes.punchline: Reference to Bender's '40% [material]' running gag
jokes.explanation: Perfect use of established Bender catchphrase in new context.

jokes.text: That's right. I'm slam dunkin'!
jokes.punchline: Successfully doing tall person activity
jokes.explanation: Straightforward payoff to height storyline. Functional but not exceptional.

jokes.text: Woo! I'm not fittin' in an airplane seat!
jokes.punchline: Celebrates an obvious disadvantage
jokes.explanation: Good ironic celebration of inconvenience. Shows Bender's skewed priorities perfectly.

jokes.text: Yeah! I'm givin' out awkward crotch hugs!
jokes.punchline: Unintentionally inappropriate height-based physical contact
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor about awkward tall/short interactions.

jokes.text: Taller, sure, but nothin' else about you changed.
jokes.setup: Bender trying to impress with new height
jokes.punchline: Brutal honesty that height doesn't fix personality
jokes.explanation: Sharp reality check that physical change doesn't equal personal growth.

jokes.text: Well, listen up, missy! I'll pick you up at eight!
jokes.setup: Angry rejection of woman's shallow standards
jokes.punchline: Immediately asking her out despite the moral stance
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender hypocrisy - principles immediately abandoned for personal gain.

jokes.text: Oop, wrong movie.
jokes.setup: Trying to find seats in movie theater
jokes.punchline: Admits he's in the wrong theater entirely
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of his disruption. Shows complete obliviousness with nice economy.

jokes.text: I think you've mistooken me for someone who knows vocabuloni.
jokes.punchline: Made-up words while claiming vocabulary ignorance
jokes.explanation: Creative Bender malapropisms while ironically proving the point. Great wordplay.

jokes.text: How do you spell that? Uh... B? No, wait. D? No, E.
jokes.punchline: Forgetting how to spell his own name
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment showing Bender's nervousness affecting basic functions.

jokes.text: Hot plus tall equals sexy? Well, you can't argue with the math.
jokes.punchline: Treating it as legitimate mathematical proof
jokes.explanation: Decent satire of celebrity culture and shallow attraction metrics.

jokes.text: Godzebo? Don't you mean Godzillow? They couldn't get the rights to Godzillow, so the writers invented an all-new and completely original monster named Godzebo!
jokes.punchline: Breaking fourth wall about copyright and lazy writing
jokes.explanation: Smart meta-commentary on copyright issues and derivative content in entertainment.

jokes.text: We can't. That was our last Tokyo.
jokes.setup: Needing to reshoot destroyed city scene
jokes.punchline: Tokyo being treated as consumable movie resource
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist joke about kaiju movie production. Original concept with perfect delivery.

jokes.text: We interrupt this soup to bring you a special report. Brought to you by... soup! Your kids deserve it, after what they did.
jokes.punchline: Ominous soup advertising with unclear child guilt
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully absurd advertising that raises disturbing questions. Great dark humor.

jokes.text: And also, isn't it pronounced Calabasas?
jokes.punchline: Correcting pronunciation during crisis
jokes.explanation: Mild humor about misplaced priorities during emergency.

jokes.text: You mean Godzebo? He means Godzillow. I mean Godzooka! The actual space dinosaur those fictional characters are based on.
jokes.punchline: Revealing the 'real' monster behind the fictional ones
jokes.explanation: Continues the meta-commentary on derivative characters with escalation.

jokes.text: We need to put a little distance between ourselves and-- What's up, guys? Don't forget to like and subscribe to--
jokes.punchline: Giant Bender doing modern social media catchphrase
jokes.explanation: Good modern reference that works in context of giant robot situation.

jokes.text: Wow. Who woulda thought Bender's head was completely empty? I guess everyone.
jokes.punchline: Realizing it's not surprising at all
jokes.explanation: Good self-correction joke that builds on established character traits.

jokes.text: Because it looks cool. Del Toro out.
jokes.punchline: Director admitting aesthetic over function, then leaving
jokes.explanation: Perfect Pacific Rim parody with great economy. Director's honest admission.

jokes.text: Wait... wasn't Bender the bad guy? Oh, loosen up, Amy.
jokes.setup: Bender being honored despite causing destruction
jokes.punchline: Dismissing valid concern about rewarding the villain
jokes.explanation: Good observation about illogical heroism narrative in movies.

jokes.text: You're dead and I'm tall! Aw, man! The D-roids wore off! And yet the butt rash is permanent!
jokes.punchline: Immediately losing height but keeping embarrassing side effect
jokes.explanation: Good payoff to pharmaceutical side effects setup with perfect timing.

jokes.text: Confidence is for losers. What I have is overconfidence.
jokes.setup: Discussion about inner qualities vs physical appearance
jokes.punchline: Bender's twisted take on positive psychology
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender philosophy - takes positive concept and makes it worse. Excellent character integration and quotability.

jokes.text: I need you to change a light bulb.
jokes.punchline: Purely practical reason for wanting him back
jokes.explanation: Nice subversion of romantic reunion with practical mundane reality.
